Dashboard.Subtitle: Pick a tool to get started.
Dashboard.PropsDesc: Turn .obj / .glb / .gltf / .fbx files into FiveM-ready .ydr props — collision and .ytyp included.
Dashboard.OptimizeDesc: Shrink mesh detail and compress textures on props, clothing, vehicles and YTDs to keep servers smooth.
Dashboard.ImageTo3DDesc: Generate a 3D model from a reference image via Meshy or other AI providers.
Dashboard.TxAdminDesc: Paste a txAdmin server log and automatically shrink the oversized streaming assets it flags.
Dashboard.RpfDesc: Pack a FiveM resource into a raw .rpf, a singleplayer ped dlc, or a client/server Replace bundle.
Dashboard.VehiclesDesc: Convert SP add-on cars (dlc.rpf or a gta5-mods link) into FiveM resources — preview in 3D, audition sounds, tune handling.
Dashboard.EmotesDesc: Convert .glb / .fbx animations or pose your own emotes — export as .ycd, resource, or dpemotes.
Dashboard.AnimDesc: Convert .glb / .fbx animations into FiveM .ycd emotes with automatic skeleton retargeting.
Dashboard.PoseDesc: Turn a single static pose into a hold-in-place FiveM emote clip.
Settings.Title: Settings
Settings.GlobalUpdateDesc: Off by default — update manually via Help → Check for updates. Turn on to prompt for updates when FiveOS starts.
Settings.OutputDesc: Pick where converted props (.ydr) and optimized resources end up. Server mode skips the zip step and drops files straight into your FiveM resource folder — choose the layout below that matches how you stream them.
Settings.SingleFolderDesc: Default destination for one-off conversions. Conversions appear here as {asset}_resource.zip. Optimize runs overwrite the source files in place, so this setting doesn't apply to them. Leave empty to use your Downloads folder.
Settings.ServerFolderDesc: If set, conversion and optimize results are written unzipped into this folder. Overrides the single-folder destination above.
Settings.ServerFolderPlaceholder: (off — leave empty to use single-folder mode)
Settings.LayoutSharedDesc: Treat the folder above as one FiveM resource. All converted stream files go into <server>/stream/ together, and fxmanifest.lua is rebuilt every run.
Settings.LayoutPerAssetDesc: Each conversion gets its own resource folder under <server>/, like {asset}_resource/stream/ with its own fxmanifest.lua — same shape as the zips, just unpacked.
Settings.ApiKeysDesc: Keys are encrypted on disk with Windows DPAPI — tied to your Windows account on this PC — and are never sent anywhere.
Settings.SketchfabHelpRun1: Used by File → Import from Sketchfab URL. Get a token at
Settings.CacheDesc: Temporary files in %TEMP%\FiveOS (3D viewer files, conversion scratch space, texture conversion work, downloads). Safe to clear at any time — your saved API keys live somewhere else and won't be touched.
About.PageDesc: FiveOS — all-in-one toolkit for converting and optimizing FiveM assets.
About.DiscordDesc: Show what you're doing in FiveOS (active tab, loaded model) on your Discord profile. Requires the Discord desktop client to be running on this PC; nothing is sent if it isn't.
Language.Desc: Choose the language used throughout FiveOS. Menus, dashboard and settings are translated; a few deep tooltips and error details may still be in English for now.
Props.OrDropFile: …or drop a file onto the viewport.
Props.SketchfabHelp: The model must be marked as downloadable on Sketchfab. You'll also need to paste a Sketchfab API token into Settings → API keys.
Crash.Title: Conversion failed
Crash.GenericSummary: The engine couldn't finish this conversion. The log below has the details.
Crash.AssimpSummary: The conversion engine couldn't load its mesh importer (assimp.dll). This almost always means your antivirus quarantined the file, or the install is incomplete. Fix: add FiveOS to your antivirus exceptions, then reinstall.
Crash.ImportSummary: No 3D geometry could be read from this file. The format may not be supported, the file may be corrupt, or it may not contain any mesh data. Try re-exporting from your 3D tool as .obj, .glb or .fbx.
